metadata.title: The Fear Engineers: How Horror Games Are Designed from the Inside
metadata.color_semantics: claude fidelity: INK #3D3929; SPARK #D97757 one accent.
metadata.author: Seth Brown
tags: The Fear Engineers: How Horror Games Are
metadata.note: HAI Fellows batch v2 — Teardown register, @NikBearBrown, Remotion animated bookends. Credit: Seth Brown — Humanitarians AI Fellow. HONESTY: all numbers trace to source article.

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. Horror games engineer fear from the inside, down to hand-placing each lamp-oil bottle. Monsters force the player into darkness, and darkness depletes the sanity meter. This ties resource scarcity directly to psychological pressure and constant threat. 'Survival horror,' first a Resident Evil marketing label in 1996, proved an accurate technical description.

artifactLines: Horror games engineer fear from the inside, down to hand-placing each lamp-oil bottle.
Monsters force the player into darkness, and darkness depletes the sanity meter.
This ties resource scarcity directly to psychological pressure and constant threat.
'Survival horror,' first a Resident Evil marketing label in 1996, proved an accurate technical description.

beats.narration_text: The mechanic that monsters force you into darkness, darkness depletes the sanity meter, and how designers placed real lamp-oil bottles by hand, showed me horror is engineered from the inside. I want to trace that loop. Help me: (1) map the full feedback loop between monster, darkness, sanity, and player resource management; (2) explain why hand-placed lamp oil matters more than procedural generation for dread; (3) unpack how 'survival horror' on the Resident Evil box became an accurate technical description of the genre.

Help me: (1) map the full feedback loop between monster, darkness, sanity, and player resource management; (2) explain why hand-placed lamp oil matters more than procedural generation for dread; (3) unpack how 'survival horror' on the Resident Evil box became an accurate technical description of the genre.
output: You have 37 bottles of lamp oil
The designers at Frictional Games knew the number
It doesn’t make monsters more likely to find you

beats.narration_text: The lamp oil is real. They placed each bottle by hand.

beats.narration_text: In 1996, nobody had a name for what Mikami built. The genre term “survival horror” appeared on the Resident Evil box itself, a marketing label that turned out to be an accurate technical description.
